seoTitle: How to Get Rounded Corners on Power BI Visuals
metaDescription: Give Power BI visuals a modern card look by rounding their corners. Follow the formatting steps to make dashboards feel cleaner and more polished.
intro: Sharp-edged visuals can make a Power BI report feel dated, while rounded corners give the same charts a softer, modern card appearance. This guide covers how to adjust the corner radius on your visuals so your dashboard looks more polished for stakeholders. It's useful whether you're building an internal dashboard or a client-facing report.

steps.heading: Select the visual you want to update
steps.body: Click on the chart or card whose corners you want to round, then open the formatting pane to access its style options.

steps.heading: Find the corner radius option
steps.body: Within the visual's formatting settings, locate the option that controls the shape of the visual's edges, sometimes grouped with border or effects settings.

steps.heading: Adjust the radius to your preference
steps.body: Increase the corner radius gradually and preview the result until you get the rounded, card-style look you want without it looking overdone.

steps.heading: Apply the style consistently across visuals
steps.body: Repeat the same radius setting on other visuals in the report so the rounded look feels intentional and consistent rather than applied to just one chart.

steps.heading: Review the final report layout
steps.body: Step back and check the overall dashboard to confirm the rounded corners improve readability and give the report a cohesive, professional appearance.

faq.question: Can I round the corners on any visual in Power BI?
faq.answer: Most standard visuals support corner radius formatting, though the exact option location can vary slightly depending on the visual type.

faq.question: Will rounded corners affect how my data is displayed?
faq.answer: No, this is a purely visual style change and doesn't affect the underlying data or calculations shown in the chart.

faq.question: How rounded should the corners be for a professional look?
faq.answer: A subtle radius usually looks the most professional, since overly rounded corners can start to look informal on business dashboards.

faq.question: Can I apply the same rounded style to every visual at once?
faq.answer: You'll generally need to set it per visual, though copying formatting between similar visuals can speed up applying it consistently.

faq.question: Does this feature require a specific Power BI license?
faq.answer: No, visual formatting options like corner radius are part of standard Power BI Desktop, not a premium-only feature.
